text: Iterated on the narrative document that pitches "living workspaces," moving it from an implementation-heavy explainer toward a coherent, inevitable-feeling story: outlined the "Common Sense to Living Workspace" arc, folded in ChatGPT critiques, and settled on framing the value around two asset types â€” knowledge assets and capability assets. Reworked the framing to open on the complexity of harnessing AI across systems, operations, people, investment, and risk, and to stress an incremental "don't rip and replace" adoption path rather than just better/faster/cheaper. Cleaned up the messy artifact names in the living-workspaces folder (e.g., renaming "Living Workspaces V3" and "Common Sense ARC Outline V2" into sensible groups) and moved loose working files into the folder root. Drafted a V3 document from the V3 outline in the same format as V2, then pushed toward a version that follows a single consultant end-to-end as a complete story. Ended with the user asking for a read-only review of an accounting-practice billing scenario (HubSpot, time tracking) to see what could be gleaned, with no changes made yet.
